title: cerinthe glabra var. auriculata
intro: Cerinthe glabra var. auriculata, also known as Honeywort, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cerinthe glabra var. auriculata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
